Text.Value: Rotary encoder single (at A and B)
Rotary encoder double (at A and B)
Rotary encoder quadruple (at A and B)
Direction (pulse at A and direction at B)
Help is not available on this subject.
Use this parameter to adjust the de-bounce timer for a digital input
Use this parameter to adjust the value format of the analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this Parameter to select input range, adjust the value format of the analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this Parameter to select output range, adjust the value format of the analogue output or to deactivate a channel
Use this parameter to adjust the value format of the analogue output or to deactivate a channel
Use this Parameter to apply a specific temperature unit on ALL channels
Use this Parameter to apply a specific frequency suppression on ALL channels
Use this parameter to select a thermocouple connected to an analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this parameter to select a temperature sensitive resistor type connected to an analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this parameter to select an input frequency band. The Counter Module can then apply a suitable filter in order to counteract signal disturbances.
Use this parameter to select a suitable counter application.
Use this Parameter to specify the use of the integrated digital output.
Use this Parameter to specify the use of the comparison Bit.
Use this parameter to specify a the use of the digital "Reset" input
Use this parameter to specify the primary use of the counter or to deactivate a counter. (If counter is deactivated all other parameters will remain dormant)
Use this parameter to specify the behaviour of the internal gate input.
Use this parameter to specify the delay between the reception of two sequential encoder values.
Use this parameter to specify the communication speed between Master and Encoder (not relevant if operation mode is set to "Listener"
Use this parameter to specify the number of bits that are to be received after the actual encoder value.
Use this parameter to specify the total number of bits to be received (Encoder Value PLUS FollowUp Bits).

Use this parameter to specify the general operation mode of the SSI Modul. The "Listening Mode" implies that the SSI Module is merely listening to the communication between another SSI Master and an SSI Encoder.The "Master Mode" implies for the Module to act as an SSI Master.
The "Shift Direction " specifies the byte order in which the encoder value is to be received.
Use this parameter to specify the edge type of the clock signal at which the encoder delivers data.
Use this parameter to specify the data format of the transmitted encoder value. (Please refer to respective Encoder DataSheet)
Use this parameter to specify if the respective process alarm should be de-activated after its first occurance ("Once ") or should always remain active.
Use this parameter to take the direction in which the encoder value is crossing a specified limit into account.
Use this parameter to swap bytes within process data words (Motorola format) or not (Intel Format).
Use this parameter to determine the behaviour of Process Output Modules in the event of loss of communication to IO Controller.
Safety watchdog time value is 0 ms (F_WD_Time)
Parameter F_SIL exceeds SIL from specific device application
Parameter F_CRC_Length does not match the generated values
Device specific diagnosis information, see manual
Profinet Device for System 1000 modular distributed I/O System
Lenze EPM-S140 Profinet Device for System 1000 System
Digital Input Module DI 2xDC24V, µs-precision ETS, Memory for TimeStamps, 15 Datasets
Digital Input Module DI 2xDC24V, µs-precision ETS, Memory for TimeStamps, 5 Datasets
Digital Output Module DO 2xDC24V , 0.5A, µs-precision ETS, Memory for TimeStamps, 15 Datasets
Digital Output Module DO 2xDC24V , 0.5A, µs-precision ETS, Memory for TimeStamps, 5 Datasets
Analogue Input Module AI 2x16Bit TC, Thermoelemente: B, C, E, J, K, L, N, R, S, T, U
Analogue Input Module AI 4x16Bit R,RTD, RTD: Pt100, Pt1000, Ni100, Ni1000
Synchron Seriel Interface (Encoder Interface) RS422 100/300/600 kBit/s
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), ASCII Protocol, 60 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), ASCII Protocol, 8 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), ASCII Protocol, 20 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), 3964(R) Protocol, 60 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), 3964(R) Protocol, 8 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), 3964(R) Protocol, 20 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), STX/ETX Protocol, 60 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), STX/ETX Protocol, 8 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), STX/ETX Protocol, 20 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), Modbus Protocol, 60 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), Modbus Protocol, 8 Byte I/O Data
Communication Module, Serial Interface (RS232), Modbus Protocol, 20 Byte I/O Data
Digital Input Modules for the Lenze System 1000
Digital Output Modules for the Lenze System 1000
Analogue Input Modules for the Lenze System 1000
Analogue Output Module for the Lenze System 1000
Technology Function Modules for the Lenze System 1000
Power Modules for the Lenze System 1000
Communication Processors for the Lenze System 1000
Rotary encoder single (at A and B)
Rotary encoder double (at A and B)
Rotary encoder quadruple (at A and B)
Direction (pulse at A and direction at B)
Help is not available on this subject.
Use this parameter to adjust the de-bounce timer for a digital input
Use this parameter to adjust the value format of the analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this Parameter to select input range, adjust the value format of the analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this Parameter to select output range, adjust the value format of the analogue output or to deactivate a channel
Use this parameter to adjust the value format of the analogue output or to deactivate a channel
Use this Parameter to apply a specific temperature unit on ALL channels
Use this Parameter to apply a specific frequency suppression on ALL channels
Use this parameter to select a thermocouple connected to an analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this parameter to select a temperature sensitive resistor type connected to an analogue input or to deactivate a channel
Use this parameter to select an input frequency band. The Counter Module can then apply a suitable filter in order to counteract signal disturbances.
Use this parameter to select a suitable counter application.
Use this Parameter to specify the use of the integrated digital output.
Use this Parameter to specify the use of the comparison Bit.
Use this parameter to specify a the use of the digital "Reset" input
Use this parameter to specify the primary use of the counter or to deactivate a counter. (If counter is deactivated all other parameters will remain dormant)
Use this parameter to specify the behaviour of the internal gate input.
Use this parameter to specify the delay between the reception of two sequential encoder values.
Use this parameter to specify the communication speed between Master and Encoder (not relevant if operation mode is set to "Listener"
Use this parameter to specify the number of bits that are to be received after the actual encoder value.
Use this parameter to specify the total number of bits to be received (Encoder Value PLUS FollowUp Bits).

Use this parameter to specify the general operation mode of the SSI Modul. The "Listening Mode" implies that the SSI Module is merely listening to the communication between another SSI Master and an SSI Encoder.The "Master Mode" implies for the Module to act as an SSI Master.
The "Shift Direction " specifies the byte order in which the encoder value is to be received.
Use this parameter to specify the edge type of the clock signal at which the encoder delivers data.
Use this parameter to specify the data format of the transmitted encoder value. (Please refer to respective Encoder DataSheet)
Use this parameter to specify if the respective process alarm should be de-activated after its first occurance ("Once ") or should always remain active.
Use this parameter to take the direction in which the encoder value is crossing a specified limit into account.
Use this parameter to swap bytes within process data words (Motorola format) or not (Intel Format).
Use this parameter to determine the behaviour of Process Output Modules in the event of loss of communication to IO Controller.
Safety watchdog time value is 0 ms (F_WD_Time)
Parameter F_SIL exceeds SIL from specific device application
Parameter F_CRC_Length does not match the generated values
